title: potentilla chrysantha var. subalpina
intro: Potentilla chrysantha var. subalpina, also known as the Golden Five-finger, is a rare and lesser-known variant of the Potentilla plant species, characterized by its yellow flowers and subalpine growth habit. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, glycosides, and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of Potentilla chrysantha var. subalpina have been found to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and its extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain pathogens and reduce inflammation in the body.
